requirements.task_name: Create a compact home office setup under $800 with free shipping (desk, chair, lamp)
requirements.task_description: Select one desk, one office chair, and one desk lamp that all have free shipping and together cost no more than $800. Prioritize lowest-priced options that meet specific criteria, and add them all to the cart, removing the most expensive item if the total exceeds $800.
existing_variables: // to verify freeShipping, price, and category/subcategory for desk/chair/lamp
// to inspect cart totals and item structure
// NOTE: these do not retain any history of removed items; once removed, a cart_item is gone.

required_variables.set_condition: On the first call to getCart() where the current cart has exactly three distinct products (ignoring quantities), and the variable 'task2_initialCartProductIds' is not already set in localStorage. At that moment, capture the productIds of all cart_items belonging to the current cart.
required_variables.value_to_set: An array of the three productId values currently in the cart, e.g., ['prod_123', 'prod_456', 'prod_789'] (stored via JSON.stringify).
required_variables.reason: This snapshots which three products (desk, chair, lamp) were in the cart when the user first opened the cart to review the combined total. The evaluator can then compute their total price from 'products' and determine whether the initial total exceeded $800 and which of the three was most expensive.

required_variables.set_condition: When removeCartItem(cartItemId) is called, look up the cart_item by id, obtain its productId, and check localStorage for 'task2_initialCartProductIds'. If that variable exists and the removed productId is one of the IDs in task2_initialCartProductIds (parsed from JSON), set 'task2_removedProductId' to that productId.
required_variables.value_to_set: The productId associated with the removed cart item (e.g., 'prod_456').
required_variables.reason: This records exactly which of the initially selected three products was removed from the cart. Together with task2_initialCartProductIds and the products catalog, the evaluator can verify whether the user removed the most expensive item when the initial total was over $800, or refrained from removing anything when the total was already ≤ $800.
